content:
Hello again, I'm not a programmer but I'm enthusiastic about the development of this driver so I'm not sure if this is the right place to ask.

I previously avoided using an NVIDIA GPU on a Linux-based environment due to my previous dissatisfaction with Nouveau and security concerns with using proprietary drivers on the Linux kernel. Things have significantly changed enough for me to invest in a AD104 GPU for my system and I'm currently enjoying using Nouveau with NVK+Zink on Gentoo GNU/Linux.

I understand that the driver stack is intended to succeed if not replace Nouveau on GSP-based GPUs, but with the out-of-tree modules and proprietary user space drivers still being more functionally complete than Nouveau, would a desktop user uninterested in CUDA, DLSS and ray-tracing still need to install the proprietary stack for the best performance and functionality once Nova is deemed ready for daily use?

Additionally, I also have concerns about the project's future under current economic conditions. If the speculated AI bubble were to burst, would it hinder the development of this driver at all?

I'm aware NDAs could prevent these questions from being answered, so I appreciate any response.
